name: Chicken with Anjou Pear Relish
recipeIngredient: Red Anjou Pear, cored and chopped (leave the skin on)
6 ounces skinless boneless chicken breast halves, apportioned
1/3 pound Idaho potatoes, scrubbed and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, finely dice the pear, radishes, and red bell pepper.
Add tarragon, basil leaves, salt, and pepper. Pulse until incorporated.
2. Do not process the almonds.
3. Combine sauce ingredients: vegetable broth, balsamic vinegar,
tarragon vinegar, and lemon juice in a bowl.
4. Preheat a square grill pan on high heat.
5. Finely julienne cut the lemon peel; press it into chicken pieces.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper, then grill until golden brown,
turning as infrequently as possible.
6. Transfer chicken to an oven-proof dish and place in the oven for 3-4 minutes.
7. In a pan, sauté the minced pear mixture until heated through.
8. Add almonds and toss gently.
9. Add sauce liquids; allow to bubble and reduce by one-third,
preserving some juiciness. Season with salt and pepper if needed.
10. Plate chicken topped with relish and sauce juice.
[260.5 CALS, 8.8 G FAT/MC est]
NOTES: Avoid making applesauce or pressing down on the relish with a spoon or spatula.
Makes enough for 3 servings. If serving 8-ounce chicken breasts,
no need to increase the amount of relish.
---MENU---
Serve with Idaho potatoes (scrubbed and cubed), sautéed in garlic, olive oil,
salt, pepper, and rosemary; roasted at 425°F for 25 minutes.
Steam fresh green beans. [360.4 CALS, 11.3 G FAT/MC est]
